concerns.text: FR-001 mandates ingesting and harmonizing GSS 2022 AND Cyberbullying Survey 2021. The tasks.md explicitly states 'GSS 2022 is NOT fetched' (T012) and implements single-dataset only. No task exists to ingest/harmonize GSS 2022 data as required by the spec.

FR-002 requires aligning demographic distributions between two independent surveys via weighting/matching. Tasks.md Phase 3 implements a 'single-dataset analysis cohort' (T014) and explicitly defers the synthetic cohort logic. No task covers the weighting/matching operation defined in FR-002.

US-1 requires generating a 'synthetic cohort dataset' from two sources. Tasks.md Phase 3 produces an 'analysis_cohort' from a single source (T016) and explicitly marks US-1 as 'Deferred/Kickback'. No task implements the US-1 scope.

SC-001 requires covariate balance ≤ 0.1 in the synthetic cohort. Since the synthetic cohort is not built (Plan deviation), and tasks.md marks SC-001 as 'Deferred/Kickback' without defining an alternative measurable success criterion for the single-dataset approach, this success criterion is untestable and uncovered.

Spec assumes GSS 2022 contains valid PCL-5 items. Plan/Tasks exclude GSS 2022 entirely. While T023 handles missing PCL-5, no task addresses the specific logic required if GSS *were* included (as per spec) but lacked PCL-5, nor does any task validate the GSS data structure as assumed in the spec.
